factors.summary: Pricing power is negatively affected as customers may shift to lower-priced products or delay purchases.

evidence.text: Moreover, the COVID-19 pandemic may delay customers’ purchasing decisions, result in a shift to lower-priced products or away from discretionary products, and may result in longer payment terms or inability to collect customer payments.
evidence.subsection: The coronavirus global pandemic could have a material adverse effect on our ability to operate, results of operations, financial condition, liquidity and ability to consummate future acquisitions.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Customers may shift to lower-priced products or delay purchases, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Labor costs may increase due to cybersecurity vulnerabilities from remote work.

evidence.text: In compliance with stay-at-home orders issued in connection with the COVID-19 pandemic, a significant subset of our employees have transitioned to working from home. As a result, more of our employees are working from locations where our cybersecurity program may be less effective and IT security may be less robust. This change may create increased vulnerability to cybersecurity incidents, including breaches of information systems security, which could result in a disruption of our operations, customer dissatisfaction, damage to our reputation and a loss of customers or revenues.
evidence.subsection: The coronavirus global pandemic could have a material adverse effect on our ability to operate, results of operations, financial condition, liquidity and ability to consummate future acquisitions.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increased vulnerability to cybersecurity incidents due to remote work could lead to higher labor-related costs.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is stable but may face disruptions if the pandemic persists.

evidence.text: While our global supply chains are currently not materially affected, it is unknown whether and to what extent they may be affected if the COVID-19 pandemic persists for an extended period.
evidence.subsection: The coronavirus global pandemic could have a material adverse effect on our ability to operate, results of operations, financial condition, liquidity and ability to consummate future acquisitions.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Supply chains are currently stable but face potential risks if the pandemic persists.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is restricted due to external factors related to the pandemic.

evidence.subsection: The coronavirus global pandemic could have a material adverse effect on our ability to operate, results of operations, financial condition, liquidity and ability to consummate future acquisitions.

sentiment.label: negative
